recall_floor_measured: 0.7339 (floor 0.90) — DETERMINISTIC, pre-existing on v0.7.2 (0.7339 identical). behavior_compat_events_pending_if_unbloc: FTS5 tokenizer upgrade (step 11) — NOTE: NOT zero-cost; carries a ~3x FTS5 query-latency regression (B2), so 'zero-surface' framing must NOT be shipped as 'zero-impact'

blockers_encountered.severity: HARD HALT (designated floor-gating test; contract: recall<0.90 → HALT rather than ship)
blockers_encountered.description: recall@10=0.7339 < 0.90. DETERMINISTIC and PRE-EXISTING (v0.7.2 yields byte-identical 0.7339). NOT a 0.8.0 regression. Root cause: ac_013b hardcodes the synthetic VaryingEmbedder (random 768-d unit vectors → ~73% sign-bit-quantization fidelity); the 0.90 floor was calibrated on the REAL bge-small embedder (~0.937, measured in the report-only operator-gated eu7). Masked in normal CI because ac_013b early-returns unless AGENT_LONG=1, which the CI verify job never sets. This is a gate/floor CALIBRATION decision, not a code fix.
blockers_encountered.resolution: UNRESOLVED — halted, routed. Orchestrator/HITL must decide: re-point ac_013b at the real embedder, re-baseline the synthetic floor, or formally elevate eu7 to the gating role and demote ac_013b to a code-shape check (ADR-level).

blockers_encountered.severity: REAL 0.8.0 REGRESSION (hard unconditional gate) — critical shippability blocker
blockers_encountered.description: p50=49.9ms > 20ms and p99=315ms > 150ms at n=100000. v0.7.2 baseline PASSES (p50=16ms/p99=56ms). ~3x latency, reproduced isolated on idle box and concurrently. Root cause: Slice-5 step-11 migration upgraded the FTS5 tokenizer to 'porter unicode61 remove_diacritics 2' (v0.7.2 used the lighter default); the porter stemmer + diacritics folding is materially more expensive at query time. The upgrade is zero-API-surface but NOT zero-cost.
blockers_encountered.resolution: UNRESOLVED — halted, routed to Slice-5/G1 band. Options for that band: cheaper tokenizer config, query-path optimization, or HITL re-ratification of the AC-012 budget at the 100k tier if the tokenizer quality gain is deemed worth the latency.

blockers_encountered.description: 8-reader concurrent=114ms > bound 75ms (~3.5x scaling vs required ~5.3x). v0.7.2 also fails (113ms > 65ms). Fails even idle. Hardware/runner parallel-scaling sensitivity (memory-bandwidth bound); perf gates are runner-pinned to canonical x86_64 tier-1 CI per the contract.
blockers_encountered.resolution: UNRESOLVED — halted. Likely a runner-pin (confirm on canonical CI perf runner) rather than a code defect; orchestrator to characterize.
